human_review_prompts: Would a parent call this polished within 5 seconds?
Can a third grader tell what to do next without teacher explanation?
Does the visual style support the learning goal rather than distract?
Would this make the teacher look organized and professionally credible if projected, shared with parents, or viewed by an administrator?
promotion_blockers: teacher controls mixed into student flow
teacher-facing output looks unfinished, childish in the wrong context, or includes debug/developer language
static_checks: CSS custom properties or equivalent design tokens present

accessibility.focus_visibility: high-contrast outline not obscured by sticky controls
accessibility.target_size: minimum 44px preferred; never below WCAG 2.2 minimum

color_system.contrast_rule: WCAG 2.2 AA minimum for text and controls
color_system.palette_strategy: calm high-contrast surfaces with bright semantic feedback

design_intent.credibility_target: parent and teacher should immediately see a polished instructional product
design_intent.desired_impression: modern playful classroom tool, not worksheet
design_intent.engagement_strategy: card-based progress, immediate visual feedback, restrained expressive accents

interaction_feedback.audio_visual_sync: speaker icons excluded from spoken text and synced to highlighted text
interaction_feedback.correct_incorrect_states: color plus icon plus text feedback
interaction_feedback.hover_focus_active: visible state changes for all controls
interaction_feedback.progress_state: current question, score, and next action visible

layout.empty_state_rule: no blank panes; every state has student-readable guidance
layout.structure: single-task cards with teacher mode separated from student mode
layout.visual_hierarchy: prompt first, action area second, supports third

mobile_first.thumb_reach_rule: primary actions reachable without horizontal scrolling

motion.purpose: confirm action and direct attention, not decorate

teacher_professional_presentation.dashboard_readability: teacher-facing status cards use at-a-glance hierarchy, plain labels, grouped insights, and clear next actions
teacher_professional_presentation.professional_language: teacher-facing copy uses concise, confident instructional language rather than placeholder, jokey, or developer-facing text
teacher_professional_presentation.shareable_output_quality: printable or screenshot-ready summaries use clean labels, readable spacing, and no embarrassing debug language
teacher_professional_presentation.teacher_mode_separation: teacher controls, answer keys, telemetry, and setup notes are visually separated from the student activity path
teacher_professional_presentation.teacher_public_credibility: polished classroom product that makes the teacher look prepared, modern, and professionally intentional when parents, administrators, or teammates see it

typography.readability_rule: large line height, short measure, no dense blocks
